text: Semantics: meaning of each section of bits

text: Example: Does the address is a route to be taken or the final destination of the message

metadata.filename: 2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf

text: Timing: when data should be sent and how fast they can be sent

text: The presentation layer is responsible for translation, compression, and encryption.

text: At each stage of the process, a PDU has a different name to reflect its new functions.

metadata.filename: 2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf

text: PDUs passing down the stack are as follows: 1. Data (Data Stream) 2. Segment 3. Packet 4. Frame 5. Bits (Bit Stream)

metadata.filename: 2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf
2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf

text: Encapsulation is a top-down process. • The level above does its process and then passes it down to the next level of the model. This process is repeated by each layer until it is sent out as a bit stream.

metadata.filename: 2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf
2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf

text: Data is de-encapsulated as it moves up the stack.

metadata.filename: 2-Protocol Architecture, TCP-IP, and Internet-Based Applications sum.pdf

text: When a layer completes its process, that layer strips off its header and passes it up to the next level to be processed. This is repeated at each layer until it is a data stream that the application can process. 1. Received as Bits (Bit Stream) 2. Frame 3. Packet 4. Segment 5. Data (Data Stream)
